title: KosDB v3.2.0 - Data Integrity & Advanced Query Features
instructions: Enhance KosDB with critical data integrity features (foreign keys, unique constraints), modern data types (JSON), advanced search capabilities (full-text), and operational improvements (metrics, query plan caching). Focus on features that improve data reliability and developer experience while maintaining backward compatibility with v3.1.0.

1783742606.1479802.instruction: Add foreign key constraint support to CREATE TABLE syntax with REFERENCES clause. Implement: 1) Syntax parsing for "column TYPE REFERENCES table(column)" in CREATE TABLE, 2) Storage of FK metadata in table schema, 3) Validation on INSERT/UPDATE operations to ensure referenced value exists, 4) Optional ON DELETE/ON UPDATE actions (CASCADE, SET NULL, RESTRICT), 5) Proper error messages for constraint violations. Update parser.py and database.py. Create test_fk_constraints.py with comprehensive tests.

log.text: Successfully implemented Foreign Key Constraints for KosDB v3.2.0: 1) Updated parser.py with comprehensive FK parsing including inline REFERENCES syntax (col TYPE REFERENCES table(col)) and table-level FOREIGN KEY syntax (FOREIGN KEY (col) REFERENCES table(col)), 2) Added support for ON DELETE/ON UPDATE actions (CASCADE, SET_NULL, RESTRICT, NO_ACTION), 3) Updated database.py with FK metadata storage in table schema, FK validation on INSERT and UPDATE operations, and proper error messages, 4) Implemented ON DELETE CASCADE (deletes referencing rows) and ON DELETE SET_NULL (sets FK to NULL), 5) Created comprehensive test suite test_fk_constraints.py with 15+ test cases covering syntax parsing, constraint validation, cascading deletes, self-referencing FKs, and edge cases. All components are backward compatible with existing v3.1.0 functionality.

1783742609.0275652.instruction: Add UNIQUE constraint support for table columns. Implement: 1) Syntax parsing for "column TYPE UNIQUE" and "UNIQUE (col1, col2)" in CREATE TABLE, 2) Automatic creation of unique indexes, 3) Validation on INSERT/UPDATE to prevent duplicate values, 4) Proper error messages for unique constraint violations, 5) Support for composite unique constraints (multiple columns). Update parser.py, database.py, and index handling. Create test_unique_constraints.py.

log.text: Successfully implemented Unique Constraints for KosDB v3.2.0: 1) Parser already supports UNIQUE syntax from FK task (both inline "col TYPE UNIQUE" and table-level "UNIQUE (col1, col2)"), 2) Updated database.py with _validate_unique_constraints() method to check for duplicates before INSERT/UPDATE, 3) Added _update_unique_indexes() method to maintain unique indexes, 4) Modified insert() to validate unique constraints and return proper error messages, 5) Modified update() to validate unique constraints (excluding current row) and update indexes, 6) Supports composite unique constraints (multiple columns), 7) NULL values are allowed in unique constraints (SQL standard), 8) Created comprehensive test suite test_unique_constraints.py with 15+ test cases covering inline/table-level syntax, composite constraints, NULL handling, error messages, and edge cases. All changes backward compatible with v3.1.0.

1783742611.97845.instruction: Add native JSON column type with querying capabilities. Implement: 1) JSON data type in CREATE TABLE schema, 2) JSON validation on INSERT/UPDATE, 3) JSON extraction operators (-> and ->>) for SELECT queries, 4) JSON functions: json_extract(), json_contains(), json_keys(), json_array_length(), 5) Index support for JSON path expressions. Update parser.py for JSON syntax, database.py for storage, and add json_functions.py module. Create test_json_type.py.

log.text: Successfully implemented JSON Data Type for KosDB v3.2.0: 1) Created json_functions.py module with comprehensive JSON utilities including validate_json(), parse_json(), json_extract(), json_extract_text(), json_contains(), json_keys(), json_array_length(), and helper functions, 2) Updated parser.py to support JSON column type in CREATE TABLE and JSON extraction operators (-> and ->>) in SELECT queries, 3) Updated database.py with column_types storage in schema, _validate_json_column() method for INSERT/UPDATE validation, modified select() to handle JSON extraction with proper aliasing, 4) JSON validation occurs on INSERT and UPDATE operations with proper error messages, 5) Supports nested JSON paths ($.store.book[0].title), text extraction (->>), JSON functions, and proper NULL handling, 6) Created comprehensive test suite test_json_type.py with 20+ test cases covering type creation, validation, extraction operators, edge cases, and JSON functions. All changes backward compatible with v3.1.0.

1783742625.7556279.instruction: Add full-text indexing and search capabilities. Implement: 1) CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX syntax for text columns, 2) Inverted index storage for efficient text search, 3) MATCH AGAINST syntax for full-text queries, 4) Search modes: natural language, boolean, and query expansion, 5) Relevance scoring and ranking of results. Create fulltext_index.py module with tokenization, stemming, and index management. Update parser.py and commands.py. Create test_fulltext_search.py.

log.text: Successfully implemented Full-Text Search for KosDB v3.2.0: 1) Created fulltext_index.py module with FulltextIndex class featuring tokenization, stemming (suffix removal rules), inverted index storage, and TF-IDF scoring, 2) Implemented FulltextIndexManager for index lifecycle (create, drop, update) and search coordination, 3) Added three search modes: NATURAL LANGUAGE (TF-IDF ranking), BOOLEAN (+include/-exclude operators), and QUERY EXPANSION (pseudo-relevance feedback), 4) Updated parser.py with CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX and DROP FULLTEXT INDEX syntax, plus MATCH ... AGAINST syntax with mode specification, 5) Updated commands.py with _create_fulltext_index(), _drop_fulltext_index(), and _match_against() command handlers, 6) Features include stop word filtering, simple stemming, relevance scoring, result highlighting, and multi-column search support, 7) Created comprehensive test suite test_fulltext_search.py with 20+ test cases covering tokenization, stemming, all search modes, index management, and edge cases. All changes backward compatible with v3.1.0.

1783742628.7421088.instruction: Add virtual table (view) support. Implement: 1) CREATE VIEW view_name AS SELECT ... syntax, 2) Storage of view definition in _system database, 3) Query execution from view definition (inline expansion), 4) DROP VIEW syntax, 5) SHOW VIEWS command, 6) Prevent circular view references. Update parser.py for view syntax, create view_manager.py module, integrate with CommandRegistry. Create test_views.py.

log.text: Successfully implemented Database Views for KosDB v3.2.0: 1) Created view_manager.py module with ViewManager class featuring view creation, storage in _system database, and query expansion, 2) Implemented circular reference detection to prevent self-referencing views and dependency cycles, 3) Added dependency tracking to prevent dropping views that other views depend on, 4) Updated parser.py with CREATE VIEW, DROP VIEW, and SHOW VIEWS syntax patterns, 5) Updated commands.py with view command handlers: _create_view(), _drop_view(), _show_views(), and integrated DESCRIBE for views, 6) View definitions stored with metadata including query, creation time, and dependencies, 7) Views support inline expansion for query execution, 8) Created comprehensive test suite test_views.py with 15+ test cases covering creation, dropping, circular references, dependencies, and edge cases. All changes backward compatible with v3.1.0.

1783742631.872813.instruction: Add execution plan caching for improved query performance. Implement: 1) Cache storage for parsed query execution plans keyed by query pattern, 2) LRU eviction policy with configurable cache size, 3) Invalidation on schema changes (ALTER, DROP, CREATE), 4) Cache statistics: hit rate, miss rate, evictions, 5) EXPLAIN CACHE command to view cache status. Update query_optimizer.py with plan cache integration. Create test_query_plan_cache.py.

log.text: Successfully implemented Query Plan Caching for KosDB v3.2.0: 1) Created PlanCache class with LRU eviction policy, configurable max size, and dependency tracking, 2) Implemented cache storage keyed by normalized query hash with table dependencies for invalidation, 3) Added cache statistics tracking: hits, misses, evictions, invalidations, hit rate, miss rate, 4) Implemented invalidation by table (removes plans depending on changed table) or all plans, 5) Updated QueryOptimizer to integrate PlanCache with automatic dependency tracking, 6) Added EXPLAIN CACHE command support in parser and commands.py, 7) Created comprehensive test suite test_query_plan_cache.py with 20+ test cases covering LRU eviction, invalidation, statistics, edge cases, and integration. All changes backward compatible with v3.1.0.

1783742634.6149855.instruction: Add Prometheus-compatible metrics and health monitoring. Implement: 1) metrics.py module with counters, gauges, and histograms for: query counts, latency, cache hit rates, connection pool stats, replication lag, 2) /metrics HTTP endpoint returning Prometheus format, 3) /health endpoint for health checks (liveness, readiness), 4) /status endpoint for detailed server status, 5) Configurable metrics collection interval and retention. Create metrics_server.py with lightweight HTTP server. Update config.json with metrics configuration. Create test_metrics.py.

log.text: Successfully implemented Prometheus-compatible Metrics and Health Monitoring for KosDB v3.2.0: 1) Created metrics.py module with Counter (monotonically increasing), Gauge (arbitrary values), and Histogram (distribution with buckets) metric types, all supporting labeled metrics, 2) Implemented MetricsRegistry for centralized metric management with thread-safe operations, 3) Created KosDBMetrics class with pre-defined metrics: query counts/latency, cache hits/misses/size, connection active/total, replication lag, storage size, table rows, and errors, 4) Created metrics_server.py with HTTP server providing /metrics (Prometheus format), /health (liveness/readiness), /health/live, /health/ready, and /status (detailed server status) endpoints, 5) Updated config.json with new 'metrics' section including host, port, collection_interval, retention_days, and configurable collectors, 6) Version bumped to 3.2.0 in config.json, 7) Created comprehensive test suite test_metrics.py with 15+ test cases covering all metric types, registry operations, KosDB-specific metrics, and edge cases. All changes backward compatible with v3.1.0.

1783742639.8384862.instruction: Add CHECK constraint support for data validation. Implement: 1) Syntax parsing for CHECK (condition) in CREATE TABLE, 2) Support for common operators: =, !=, <, >, <=, >=, IN, BETWEEN, LIKE, IS NULL, 3) Validation on INSERT and UPDATE operations, 4) Proper error messages with constraint name, 5) Storage of CHECK constraints in table schema. Update parser.py and database.py. Create test_check_constraints.py.

1783742643.7272708.instruction: Enhance ALTER TABLE with comprehensive schema modifications. Implement: 1) ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN with full type support and constraints, 2) ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN with CASCADE handling for dependent objects, 3) ALTER TABLE MODIFY COLUMN for type changes, 4) ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN, 5) ALTER TABLE ADD/DROP INDEX, 6) ALTER TABLE ADD/DROP CONSTRAINT (FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, CHECK). Update parser.py for ALTER syntax, database.py for schema modification. Ensure data integrity during column modifications. Create test_alter_table.py.

1783742647.0765712.instruction: Add subquery capabilities to SELECT statements. Implement: 1) Scalar subqueries in SELECT and WHERE clauses, 2) IN/NOT IN subqueries with single column results, 3) EXISTS/NOT EXISTS subqueries, 4) Correlated subqueries with outer reference resolution, 5) Subquery optimization with semi-join transformation where applicable. Update parser.py for subquery syntax, query_optimizer.py for execution planning. Create test_subqueries.py.

1783742649.6556873.instruction: Update all documentation for new v3.2.0 features. Implement: 1) Update README.md with new features and examples, 2) Update CHANGELOG.md with v3.2.0 entry, 3) Create MIGRATION_v3.1_to_v3.2.md guide, 4) Update README_CONFIG.md with new configuration options, 5) Add SQL reference documentation for new syntax, 6) Update examples/ with new feature demonstrations. Ensure all examples are tested and working.
